metadata.title: Doppler VPN — Secure No-Logs VPN & Proxy for Privacy
metadata.description: Doppler VPN keeps you private with VLESS-Reality proxy encryption, DNS protection, and a strict no-logs policy. Bypasses DPI and government censorship. Available on iOS, Android, macOS & Windows.

hero.subheadline: Built on VLESS-Reality — your traffic looks like regular HTTPS. Undetectable by deep packet inspection. No registration, no logs, no tracking.

features.title: Why Doppler Is the Best VPN for Privacy
features.subtitle: Most VPNs ask for your email before protecting your privacy. Doppler doesn't.

noRegistration.title: No-Registration VPN
noRegistration.description: Your device serves as your account. No email. No phone number. No personal information. Private VPN access begins the moment you connect.

vlessReality.title: VLESS-Reality Encryption
vlessReality.description: Advanced anti-censorship VPN protocol that makes your traffic look like regular HTTPS. Undetectable by deep packet inspection, fast, and reliable in restricted networks.

smartRouting.title: Smart VPN Routing
smartRouting.description: Intelligent traffic routing by country. Automatically selects the best VPN server path for speed and reliability based on your destination.

adBlocker.title: Built-in Ad Blocker
adBlocker.description: DNS-level ad blocking reduces around 50% of ads and trackers. Cleaner browsing with less clutter, built right into your VPN.

minimalData.title: Strict No-Logs Policy
minimalData.description: We don't log your browsing activity. We don't track websites you visit. A truly private VPN with minimal data collection by design.

dnsProtection.title: Private DNS Protection
dnsProtection.description: Encrypted DNS queries keep your browsing private. Blocks trackers and malware domains at the DNS level — works across all your apps on Android, iOS, and desktop.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Doppler VPN

what.question: What is Doppler VPN?
what.answer: Doppler VPN is a privacy-focused virtual private network service that encrypts your internet connection and hides your IP address. It includes built-in ad blocking, content filtering by category, and support for custom blocklists - all without requiring registration.

noLogs.question: Is Doppler VPN really no-logs?
noLogs.answer: Yes, we operate a strict no-logs policy. We don't track, store, or share any of your browsing activity, connection timestamps, IP addresses, or any other personal data. Your privacy is our priority.

adBlocker.question: How does the ad blocker work?
adBlocker.answer: Our ad blocker works at the DNS level, blocking ads, trackers, and malicious domains before they even load. This means faster browsing, less data usage, and protection across all your apps - not just your browser.

categories.question: What content categories can I filter?
categories.answer: You can filter content across multiple categories including adult content, gambling, social media, gaming, streaming, and more. You can also import custom blocklists for additional content filtering control.

devices.question: How many devices can I use?
devices.answer: A single Doppler VPN subscription covers up to 10 devices simultaneously. Use it on your phones, tablets, and computers - all protected at once.

platforms.question: What platforms are supported?
platforms.answer: Doppler VPN is available on iOS (iPhone & iPad), macOS, and Android. We're constantly working on expanding our platform support.

cancel.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
cancel.answer: You can cancel your subscription anytime through your device's app store (App Store for iOS/macOS or Google Play for Android). Your access continues until the end of your billing period. You will not be charged after cancellation.

trial.question: Is there a free trial?
trial.answer: Yes! Every new user gets a 3-day free trial with full access to all Pro features. We also offer a 30-day money-back guarantee on all plans.

whatIsIncluded.question: What is included in Premium?
whatIsIncluded.answer: Premium includes VPN servers in 50+ locations with VLESS-Reality encryption, DNS-level ad blocker that works across all apps, content filter with parental controls, and connection on up to 10 devices simultaneously.

plans.question: What subscription plans are available?
plans.answer: We offer three plans: Monthly, 6-Month, and Yearly. All plans include the same Premium features. Longer plans offer a lower per-month price. Subscriptions auto-renew unless canceled at least 24 hours before the end of the billing period.

restore.question: I subscribed but my features aren't working
restore.answer: Open the app, go to Profile > Subscription and tap Restore Purchases. This will re-sync your subscription with our servers. If the issue persists, contact our support team at support@simnetiq.store or contact @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram.

refund.question: How do I request a refund?
refund.answer: Refunds are processed by Apple or Google. For iOS, visit reportaproblem.apple.com and sign in with your Apple ID. For Android, open Google Play > Account > Order history. Refunds are typically processed within 48 hours.

doppler.subtitle: Secure, private VPN with no registration. Connect instantly with VLESS-Reality encryption and DNS protection.

footer.description: Privacy-first VPN with advanced ad blocking and content filtering.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At Doppler VPN, your privacy is our top priority. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle your information when you use our VPN, ad blocking, and content filtering services.

noLogs.title: No-Logs Policy
noLogs.content: We operate a strict no-logs policy. We do not track, store, or share any of the following: your browsing activity, connection timestamps, originating IP addresses, DNS queries, bandwidth usage, or any other data that could identify you or your online activities. VPN traffic is encrypted end-to-end and is never inspected, logged, or stored.

collect.title: What We Collect
collect.content: We only collect the minimum information necessary to provide our service: a randomly generated device identifier (not linked to your personal identity), payment information (processed securely by Apple App Store or Google Play — we never see your payment details), anonymous aggregated server load statistics to maintain performance, and your email address only if you voluntarily contact support.

vpnData.title: VPN Data Handling
vpnData.content: When you connect to our VPN service, your internet traffic is encrypted using the VLESS-Reality protocol and routed through our servers. We do not inspect, log, store, modify, or sell any data that passes through our VPN tunnel. Our DNS-level ad blocker and content filter operate on our DNS resolver and do not log individual queries or browsing patterns.

security.title: Data Security
security.content: We use the VLESS-Reality protocol to protect your connection, making your traffic indistinguishable from regular HTTPS. All data transmission between your device and our servers is encrypted end-to-end. Our server infrastructure is configured to minimize data retention by design.

thirdParty.title: Third Parties
thirdParty.content: We do not sell, trade, or share your personal information with third parties. We use RevenueCat for subscription management (they process only anonymous purchase identifiers) and Apple/Google app stores for payment processing. These providers are bound by their own privacy policies and contractual obligations.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.content: Our content filtering feature includes parental control capabilities. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. The content filter operates at the DNS level without collecting or storing any personal data about users of any age.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at support@simnetiq.store

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using Doppler VPN, you agree to these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully.

service.title: Service Description
service.content: Doppler VPN provides a virtual private network service that encrypts your internet connection and hides your IP address for privacy purposes. The service also includes DNS-level ad blocking and content filtering with parental controls. The service is provided "as is" and "as available."

usage.title: Acceptable Use
usage.content: You agree to use Doppler VPN only for lawful purposes and in compliance with all applicable local, national, and international laws. You may not use the service for any illegal activities, to harm others, to distribute malware, to infringe on intellectual property rights, or to interfere with our service operations. We reserve the right to terminate accounts that violate these terms.

subscription.title: Subscriptions & Payments
subscription.content: Doppler VPN Premium includes access to premium VPN servers in 50+ locations, DNS-level ad blocking, content filtering with parental controls, and connection on up to 10 devices simultaneously. Subscriptions are billed through the Apple App Store or Google Play Store. Plans are available on a monthly, 6-month, or annual basis and auto-renew unless canceled at least 24 hours before the end of the current billing period. You can cancel your subscription at any time through your device's app store settings. Refunds are subject to the respective app store's refund policies.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Doppler VPN shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the service.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. We will notify you of any material changes through the app or via email. Continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at support@simnetiq.store

guide.title: Setup Guide
guide.subtitle: Get connected in minutes. Choose your device to begin.

android.title: Android Setup
android.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your Android device
android.step1Desc: Download the Doppler VPN APK directly from our website. No Play Store needed.
android.step2Desc: Open the app, create an account, and subscribe to a Pro plan. You can also subscribe via our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot or on the website.
android.step3Desc: Pick a server location from the list. Doppler connects using the fast and secure VLESS-Reality protocol.
android.step4Desc: Tap Connect and allow VPN permission when prompted. You're now protected!
android.troubleshoot1: If connection fails, try a different server location.
android.troubleshoot2: Make sure Doppler VPN has battery optimization disabled in your Android settings.
android.troubleshoot3: For help, message @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram — our AI support is available 24/7.

ios.title: iOS Setup
ios.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your iPhone or iPad
ios.step1Desc: Install Doppler VPN from the App Store. It's free to download.
ios.step2Desc: Open the app, create an account, and subscribe to a Pro plan via in-app purchase. You can also subscribe via our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot or on the website.
ios.step3Desc: Pick a server location from the list. Doppler connects using the fast and secure VLESS-Reality protocol.
ios.step4Desc: Tap Connect and allow VPN permission when prompted. Your traffic is now encrypted and protected!
ios.troubleshoot1: If the VPN won't connect, try a different server location.
ios.troubleshoot2: Ensure Doppler VPN has permission to add VPN configurations in Settings > General > VPN.
ios.troubleshoot3: For help, message @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram — our AI support is available 24/7.
ios.dlMacStoreDesc: Runs natively on M-chip Macs via Mac App Store

windows.title: Windows Setup
windows.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your Windows PC
windows.step1Desc: Download v2rayN from GitHub. Extract the ZIP file and run v2rayN.exe.
windows.step2Desc: Open our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot, tap "Connect VPN", choose a server, and you'll receive a VLESS config link.
windows.step3Desc: Copy the VLESS link. In v2rayN, click "Server" → "Import from clipboard" or press Ctrl+V.
windows.step4Desc: Right-click the v2rayN tray icon and select "System proxy" → "Set as system proxy". You're connected!
windows.troubleshoot1: Make sure Windows Defender or your antivirus isn't blocking v2rayN.
windows.troubleshoot3: For help, message @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram — our AI support is available 24/7.

mac.title: macOS Setup
mac.subtitle: Set up Doppler VPN on your Mac
mac.step1Desc: Download V2RayXS from GitHub or install Streisand from the Mac App Store.
mac.step2Desc: Open our Telegram bot @dopplercreatebot, tap "Connect VPN", choose a server, and you'll receive a VLESS config link.
mac.step3Desc: Copy the VLESS link. Open V2RayXS → "Import" → "From clipboard". Or in Streisand, tap + → "Import from clipboard".
mac.step4Desc: Click the connect button. Allow the VPN configuration when macOS prompts you. Done!
mac.troubleshoot1: If macOS blocks the app, go to System Preferences > Privacy & Security and allow it.
mac.troubleshoot2: Try restarting the app if the connection drops.
mac.troubleshoot3: For help, message @DopplerSupportBot on Telegram — our AI support is available 24/7.

what.question: What is Doppler VPN?
what.answer: Doppler VPN is a fast, private VPN service that uses VLESS-Reality technology. It works on iOS, Android, macOS, and Windows.

cost.question: How much does it cost?
cost.answer: Doppler Pro costs $6.99/month, $29.99/6 months (save 28%), or $39.99/year (save 52%). One-time payments, no auto-renewal. Prices before tax.

subscribe.question: How do I subscribe?
subscribe.answer: Visit our subscribe page, enter your email, choose a plan, and pay via Stripe. Your Pro subscription activates instantly.

multiDevice.question: Can I use one account on multiple devices?
multiDevice.answer: Yes! Doppler Pro supports up to 10 devices on a single account.

accountId.question: What's my Account ID?
accountId.answer: Your Account ID (VPN-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX) is your key across all devices. It's created when you first use the app or subscribe on the web.

refund.question: Do you offer refunds?
refund.answer: Yes, we offer a 30-day money-back guarantee. Contact support to request a refund.

privacy.question: Is my data private?
privacy.answer: Absolutely. Doppler VPN has a strict no-logs policy. We don't collect or store your browsing data.

platforms.question: Which platforms are supported?
platforms.answer: iOS, Android, macOS (native Doppler app) and Windows (via v2rayN client).

wontConnect.question: VPN won't connect
wontConnect.answer: Try switching servers. Go to Settings → select a different server. If the issue persists, restart the app.

drops.question: Connection drops frequently
drops.answer: Enable Always-On VPN in Settings. Also check your internet connection.

battery.question: Battery drain on mobile
battery.answer: Some battery usage is normal. Disable Always-On if you only need VPN occasionally.

noSub.question: App says 'No subscription'
noSub.answer: Make sure you're using the correct Account ID. If you just purchased, wait a few seconds and restart the app.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Your Account
deleteAccount.warning: Account deletion is permanent. All data, subscription time, and server configurations will be removed. This action cannot be undone.
deleteAccount.alternative: If you can't access the app, contact support to request account deletion.

guideProtocols.title: VPN Protocols
guideProtocols.subtitle: Doppler uses VLESS-Reality — a next-generation protocol designed for censorship resistance and undetectable privacy. Here's why.
guideProtocols.comparison: Why VLESS-Reality over traditional VPN protocols

vless.title: VLESS-Reality
vless.description: An advanced protocol designed to bypass deep packet inspection (DPI) and network censorship. VLESS-Reality disguises VPN traffic as regular HTTPS, making it nearly undetectable — this is what powers every Doppler connection.
vless.stealth: Undetectable traffic — looks like normal HTTPS

whyNotWireguard.title: Why not WireGuard?
whyNotWireguard.desc: WireGuard is a great protocol for speed, but it has a known packet structure that makes it easy to detect and block. In countries with internet censorship (Russia, China, Iran), WireGuard connections are blocked instantly by DPI systems. VLESS-Reality solves this by producing traffic that's indistinguishable from regular HTTPS.

dopplerAdvantage.title: The Doppler advantage
dopplerAdvantage.desc: By building exclusively on VLESS-Reality, every Doppler connection is censorship-resistant by default. Download the app, connect, and your traffic is invisible to DPI systems — no manual configuration needed.

cancel.title: Cancel subscription
cancel.desc: For App Store: Settings → Apple ID → Subscriptions → Doppler VPN → Cancel. For Google Play: Google Play → Account → Subscriptions → Cancel. Your access continues until the end of the billing period.

switch.title: Switch plans
switch.desc: You can upgrade or downgrade your plan at any time through the same platform you subscribed on. The change takes effect at the next billing cycle.

crossPlatform.title: Cross-platform subscriptions
crossPlatform.desc: All subscriptions work across every platform. A subscription purchased via the Telegram bot works in the Doppler app on iOS and Mac, and vice versa. Your account is tied to your device, not the purchase method.

servers.title: Global Server Network
servers.subtitle: Choose from multiple locations worldwide. All servers support dual-protocol architecture.

deleteAccount.title: Delete Your Account
deleteAccount.metaDescription: Learn how to delete your Doppler VPN account and what data gets removed.
deleteAccount.intro: At Doppler VPN, we respect your right to control your data. You can delete your account and all associated data at any time directly from the app.

howToDelete.title: How to Delete Your Account
howToDelete.content:
1. Open the Doppler VPN app on your device.
2. Go to Settings.
3. Tap "Delete Account".
4. Confirm the deletion.

Your account and all associated data will be permanently deleted immediately. This action cannot be undone.

whatGetsDeleted.title: What Data Gets Deleted
whatGetsDeleted.content:
When you delete your account, the following data is permanently removed from our servers:

• Your account ID (VPN-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX)
• All registered device sessions
• Linked contact information (email or Telegram, if provided)
• Subscription records and transaction history
• All account preferences and settings

whatWeRetain.title: Data Retention After Deletion
whatWeRetain.content:
Doppler VPN does not retain any personally identifiable data after account deletion. Anonymized, aggregated usage statistics (such as total user counts) may be retained but cannot be linked back to your account.

Purchase records managed by Google Play or Apple may be retained by those platforms according to their own privacy policies.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.content: If you have any questions about data deletion or need assistance, contact us at support@dopplervpn.org.

technicalHowItWorks.title: How Doppler VPN Protects Your Traffic
technicalHowItWorks.subtitle: Your connection is encrypted and routed through Doppler's edge network. Nothing is logged.

step1.title: Your Device

step2.title: VLESS-Reality Tunnel

step4.title: Open Internet

useCases.title: Who Uses Doppler
useCases.subtitle: Built for people who need more than a generic VPN.

restricted.title: People in Restricted Networks
restricted.description: Connect freely when your government or ISP blocks VPN traffic. Doppler's protocol bypasses active censorship.

travelers.title: Travelers on Public Wi-Fi
travelers.description: Hotel, airport, cafe — your traffic is encrypted and your DNS queries are private. No one on the network can see what you do.

journalists.title: Journalists & Researchers
journalists.description: Access blocked sources and communicate without exposing your traffic patterns. No account means no identity trail.

developers.title: Developers & Remote Workers
developers.description: Reliable encrypted tunnels that don't get throttled or blocked. Works consistently across restrictive corporate and national firewalls.

comparisonTable.title: Doppler vs. Traditional VPNs
comparisonTable.subtitle: Not all VPNs are built the same.

censorshipResistance.title: Built to Bypass Government Censorship
censorshipResistance.subtitle: Doppler defeats TSPU, DPI, TLS fingerprinting, and active probing — the same systems Russia, China, and Iran use to block VPNs.

dpi.title: Defeats Deep Packet Inspection
dpi.description: TSPU and DPI hardware use machine learning to detect VPN traffic patterns. VLESS-Reality produces zero signature — there's nothing to detect.

tls.title: Real TLS Handshake
tls.description: Doppler performs a genuine TLS handshake with a real website certificate. DPI sees a normal HTTPS connection, not a VPN tunnel.

fingerprint.title: Passes Active Probing
fingerprint.description: When censors probe our servers, they respond as legitimate websites. Only authenticated clients activate the VPN tunnel.

regions.title: Blocked by Russia — Still Operational
regions.description: Our domain is on Roskomnadzor's blocklist. We're still here. Download via Telegram bot when the website is blocked.

metadata.title: Internet Censorship Guide — How VPNs Get Blocked & How to Stay Connected
metadata.description: Technical guide on how governments block VPNs and proxies using DPI, TSPU, and ML — and how VLESS-Reality proxy bypasses every detection method. Prepare for escalating restrictions.

hero.title: Your Guide to Internet Freedom
hero.subtitle: How governments detect and block VPNs, how Doppler defeats every method, and what you should do now to stay connected as restrictions escalate.

detection.title: How Governments Detect and Block VPNs
detection.intro: Internet censorship has evolved far beyond simple IP blocking. Countries like Russia, China, and Iran deploy sophisticated systems that use machine learning to identify and terminate VPN connections in real time. Here's exactly how they do it.

tspu.title: The Infrastructure: TSPU and DPI
tspu.p1: Russia deploys TSPU (Technical Systems for Threat Countermeasures) — specialized hardware installed directly on ISP network nodes. China uses the Great Firewall, Iran operates NIN. These systems perform Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) on every packet crossing the network.
tspu.p2: Traditional VPN protocols like OpenVPN, WireGuard, and IPSec have distinct packet structures. DPI identifies them instantly and drops the connection. Even "obfuscated" modes often fail because the underlying handshake pattern remains detectable.
tspu.p3: When basic signature matching isn't enough, these systems activate machine learning modules. They stop looking inside packets (everything is encrypted) and start analyzing how the traffic behaves.

method1.title: Method 1: TLS Client Fingerprinting
method1.description: Every TLS client — Chrome, Firefox, Safari — sends connection parameters in a specific order during the handshake. This creates a unique fingerprint. A VPN client pretending to be HTTPS traffic often sends these parameters in a different order than any real browser. DPI compares the fingerprint against a database of known browsers. Mismatch? Flagged as a tunnel.

method2.title: Method 2: Traffic Entropy Analysis
method2.description: Real internet usage has distinctive patterns. Video streaming produces many large sequential packets. Web browsing follows a request-response-pause cycle. A VPN tunnel has a completely different entropy profile — uniform packet sizes, consistent timing, non-human density. Even when traffic is disguised as HTTPS, machine learning models detect these statistical anomalies in packet lengths and time delays.

method3.title: Method 3: Active Probing
method3.description: When the system suspects a foreign server is running a VPN, it sends its own probe request. If the server responds like a normal website — returning a 404 error or an HTML page — it passes. But if the server attempts to initiate a VPN handshake or behaves unexpectedly, the IP address is immediately banned. Simple VPNs fail this test instantly.

method4.title: Method 4: IP and DNS Blacklisting
method4.description: Known VPN provider IP ranges are blocked outright. DNS requests for VPN domains are intercepted. Commercial VPN providers with public server lists are the easiest targets — their entire infrastructure can be blocked in minutes.

detection.conclusion: The result: push-button VPNs and standard proxy services like NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark stop working within hours of a crackdown. Only proxy protocols specifically engineered to be indistinguishable from regular web traffic survive.

defeats.title: How Doppler Defeats Every Detection Method
defeats.subtitle: Each detection technique has a specific countermeasure built into VLESS-Reality — the protocol that powers every Doppler connection.

signatures.description: VLESS-Reality produces no identifiable protocol pattern. There is no VPN handshake, no distinctive packet structure, nothing for DPI signature databases to match against.

fingerprinting.description: Reality performs a real TLS handshake using a legitimate website's certificate. The fingerprint matches exactly what a real browser would produce — because it is a real TLS connection.

entropy.description: Traffic flowing through a VLESS-Reality tunnel is formatted identically to standard HTTPS. Packet sizes, timing, and flow patterns match normal web browsing, defeating statistical analysis.

probing.description: When probed, Doppler servers respond exactly as the legitimate website they mirror. Unauthorized probe requests receive normal web responses. The VPN tunnel only activates with valid client authentication.

blacklisting.description: Doppler rotates server endpoints and IP addresses. Even if one endpoint is identified, the network adapts automatically. Our Telegram bot provides instant access to updated server configurations.

blocked.title: Blocked in Russia — And Still Here
blocked.p1: Russian authorities have specifically targeted and blocked Doppler VPN. Our domain has been added to the federal blocklist. This isn't something we hide — it's proof that our technology works.
blocked.p2: Unlike commercial VPN providers that comply with takedown requests or quietly exit restricted markets, Doppler was built for this exact scenario. When our website gets blocked, our Telegram bot remains accessible. When server IPs get banned, our infrastructure rotates automatically.
blocked.p3: Every block they impose, we bypass. That's not a marketing claim — it's our daily operation.

future.title: What to Expect: The Future of Internet Restrictions
future.intro: Internet censorship isn't standing still. Understanding where things are headed helps you prepare. Here's the realistic progression based on current infrastructure and policy trends.

stage1.label: Current Stage
stage1.title: DPI + Machine Learning Detection
stage1.description: Governments deploy TSPU/DPI hardware with ML modules on ISP nodes. Standard VPN protocols are blocked automatically. VLESS-Reality and similar anti-censorship protocols still work because they produce traffic indistinguishable from HTTPS.
stage1.status: Active now in Russia, China, Iran

stage2.label: Possible Next Step
stage2.title: Whitelist Model
stage2.description: Instead of blocking specific protocols, only approved domains and IP addresses would be accessible. Everything else is blocked by default. SSH, RDP, gaming ports — all blocked unless registered by a legal entity. This was partially tested during Russia's 2018 Telegram ban.

stage3.label: Extreme Scenario
stage3.title: National Internet Isolation
stage3.description: A fully isolated domestic network with mandatory state-issued root certificates, local mirrors of foreign services, and no direct access to the global internet. Think North Korea's Kwangmyong, but at scale.

reality.title: The Economic Reality Check
reality.p1: In 2018, when Roskomnadzor tried to block just Telegram, they ended up banning millions of Amazon and Google IP addresses. Cash registers in stores stopped working. Smart home devices went offline. Banking push notifications failed.
reality.p2: A whitelist model would cause this at 100x scale. Modern Russian websites depend on Google Fonts, foreign CDN libraries, international certificate authorities, and cloud services. Banks and logistics companies use foreign SSL certificates. Developers would lose access to GitHub, Docker Hub, and npm.
reality.p3: Full isolation is technically possible — but its price is technological degradation. Windows and Android updates become unavailable. Medical equipment loses driver support. AI development stops entirely without global open-source infrastructure.
reality.p4: The most likely path is gradual restriction over years, not a sudden cutoff. But each escalation step means more VPN users get left behind — unless they're prepared.

prepare.title: How to Prepare Yourself Now
prepare.subtitle: Don't wait until access gets harder. Take these steps today.

step1.title: Download Doppler VPN Now
step1.description: Install the app while app stores and our website are still accessible in your region. Once restrictions escalate, downloading becomes significantly harder.

step2.title: Save Our Telegram Bot
step2.description: Add @dopplercreatebot to your Telegram. Even if our website gets blocked, the bot remains accessible inside Telegram and can provide you with VPN configurations instantly.

step3.title: Get the Standalone Android APK
step3.description: Our Telegram bot distributes the Android app directly as an APK file — no Play Store required. This is your backup if Google Play becomes restricted in your country.

step4.title: Keep Your App Updated
step4.description: We push protocol updates to stay ahead of new blocking techniques. Enable auto-updates or check for updates regularly through our Telegram channels.

step5.title: Share With People Who Need It
step5.description: The more people have Doppler installed before restrictions escalate, the better. Share our Telegram bot link — it's the most censorship-resistant distribution channel we have.

cta.title: We're Not Leaving Anyone Behind
cta.subtitle: Doppler VPN was built specifically for restricted networks. We continuously improve our infrastructure, rotate servers, and update protocols to stay ahead of every new blocking technique. Your internet freedom is not negotiable.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: Doppler VPN was created with a simple goal: give everyone access to an open, private internet — regardless of where they live. Traditional VPNs are easily detected and blocked by governments and ISPs. Our VLESS-Reality protocol solves this by making VPN traffic look like regular web browsing, ensuring your connection stays private and accessible.
